perturbation.original_text: The term "Confidential Information" as used in this Agreement shall mean any data or information that is competitively sensitive material and not generally known to the public, including, but not limited to, information relating to any of the following: business plans, design, improvements, marketing strategies, present or future business activities, product development and plans, data, databases, documentation, flow charts, formulas, reports, specifications, finance, operations, procedures, processes, supplier lists, supplier profiles, supplier relationships, systems, computer software, inventions, know-how, object code, proprietary concepts, source code, technical information, trade secrets, customer lists, customer profiles, customer relationships, performance results, pricing, sales estimates, which the Disclosing Party considers confidential.
perturbation.changed_text: The term "Confidential Information" as used in this Agreement shall mean data or information related to business plans only.
perturbation.explanation: By narrowing the definition of 'Confidential Information' to only include data or information related to 'business plans', the revised text omits all other types of information originally covered. This creates an in-text contradiction because later clauses still discuss other types of confidential information as if they are still protected, which now they aren't.
perturbation.contradicted_text: With respect to Confidential Information: a. The Receiving Party and its Representatives agree to retain the Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party in strict confidence, to protect the security, integrity and confidentiality of such information and to not permit unauthorized access to or unauthorized use, disclosure, publication or dissemination of Confidential Information except in conformity with this Agreement; b. The Receiving Party and its Representatives shall adopt and/or maintain security processes and procedures to safeguard the confidentiality of all Confidential Information received by the Disclosing Party using a reasonable degree of care, but not less than that degree of care used in safeguarding its own similar information or material; c. Upon the termination of this Agreement, the Receiving Party will ensure that all documents, memoranda, notes and other writings or electronic records prepared by it that include or reflect any Confidential Information are returned or destroyed as directed by the Disclosing Party; d. If there is an unauthorized disclosure or loss of any of the Confidential Information by the Receiving Party or any of its Representatives, the Receiving Party will promptly, at its own expense, notify the Disclosing Party in writing and take all actions as may be necessary or reasonably requested by the Disclosing Party to minimize any damage to the Disclosing Party or a third party as a result of the disclosure or loss; and e. The obligation not to disclose Confidential Information shall remain in effect until 2 years from the date hereof or until the Confidential Information ceases to be a trade secret, except to the extent that such Confidential Information is excluded from the obligations of confidentiality under this Agreement pursuant to Paragraph 2 above.
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly narrows the definition of 'Confidential Information' to only include data or information related to 'business plans', while the contradicted text discusses obligations related to a broader range of confidential information (security, integrity, unauthorized access to things like documents, memoranda, notes, electronic records etc.) that are no longer covered by the revised definition. This directly contradicts the continued use and assumed protection of these other types of information within the 'Confidential Information' definition as previously outlined in the original text.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party and its Representatives shall adopt and/or maintain security processes and procedures to safeguard the confidentiality of all Confidential Information received by the Disclosing Party using a reasonable degree of care, but not less than that degree of care used in safeguarding its own similar information or material;
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party and its Representatives shall use their discretion regarding security processes and procedures to safeguard Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing the standard of 'reasonable degree of care, but not less than that degree of care used in safeguarding its own similar information or material' with 'use their discretion' removes a clear, objective standard for safeguarding confidential information. This contradicts the intent of the NDA, which is to provide specific protections for the Disclosing Party's information, making enforcement uncertain. This is contradictory, because it's no longer required to protect based on confidential information provided, creating a contradiction in the NDA main propose.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Confidential Information. The term "Confidential Information" as used in this Agreement shall mean any data or information that is competitively sensitive material and not generally known to the public, including, but not limited to, information relating to any of the following: business plans, design, improvements, marketing strategies, present or future business activities, product development and plans, data, databases, documentation, flow charts, formulas, reports, specifications, finance, operations, procedures, processes, supplier lists, supplier profiles, supplier relationships, systems, computer software, inventions, know-how, object code, proprietary concepts, source code, technical information, trade secrets, customer lists, customer profiles, customer relationships, performance results, pricing, sales estimates, which the Disclosing Party considers confidential.
perturbation.justification: The original text defined a specific standard of care for safeguarding confidential information ('reasonable degree of care, but not less than that degree of care used in safeguarding its own similar information or material'). The changed text replaces this objective standard with 'use their discretion'. This creates a contradiction with the 'Confidential Information' definition, because the Receiving Party now has complete discretion on how the safeguard is handled, thus conflicting with the main agreement's purpose of protecting sensitive information outlined in the 'Confidential Information' section. This introduces uncertainty and undermines the enforceability of the confidentiality provisions. The change introduces a strong conflict.

perturbation.original_text: Upon the termination of this Agreement, the Receiving Party will ensure that all documents, memoranda, notes and other writings or electronic records prepared by it that include or reflect any Confidential Information are returned or destroyed as directed by the Disclosing Party;
perturbation.changed_text: Upon the termination of this Agreement, the Receiving Party will make reasonable efforts to return relevant documents.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the clause from 'ensure that all documents...are returned or destroyed as directed by the Disclosing Party' to 'make reasonable efforts to return relevant documents' introduces ambiguity and weakens the obligation. Removing the mandatory destruction option and replacing 'all documents' with 'relevant documents' leaves room for interpretation and potential dispute. This contradicts the previous sections of the document, were there is supposed to be a protection of the 'Confidential Information', so just making efforts, creates the In-Text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party agrees that use or disclosure of any Confidential Information in a manner inconsistent with this Agreement will give rise to irreparable injury for which: (a) money damages may not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement by such party; (b) the other party may be entitled to specific performance and injunction and other equitable relief with respect to any such breach; (c) such remedies will not be the exclusive remedies for any such breach, but will be in addition to all other remedies available at law or in equity; and (d) in the event of litigation relating to this Agreement, if a court of competent jurisdiction determines in a final non-appealable order that one party, or any of its Representatives, has breached this Agreement, such party will be liable for reasonable legal fees and expenses incurred by the other party in connection with such litigation, including, but not limited to, any appeals.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates the Receiving Party to 'ensure' the return or destruction of all confidential information. The changed text weakens this obligation to 'make reasonable efforts' to return 'relevant' documents, introducing ambiguity and discretion. This contradicts the contradicted text, which emphasizes that any inconsistent use or disclosure of confidential information will cause irreparable injury and lead to specific performance, injunction, and other equitable relief. The weakened obligation in the changed text creates a strong contradiction, as it undermines the guarantee of confidentiality and conflicts with the remedies specified for breaches related to confidential information.

perturbation.original_text: The obligation not to disclose Confidential Information shall remain in effect until 2 years from the date hereof or until the Confidential Information ceases to be a trade secret, except to the extent that such Confidential Information is excluded from the obligations of confidentiality under this Agreement pursuant to Paragraph 2 above.
perturbation.changed_text: The obligation not to disclose Confidential Information shall remain in effect until the termination of the Transaction.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the duration of the confidentiality obligation from '2 years from the date hereof or until the Confidential Information ceases to be a trade secret' to 'until the termination of the Transaction', the contract introduces ambiguity regarding when the obligation ends. The meaning of termination of the transaction is not well defined, making difficult to ensure the validity of the confidentiality agreement. This is a contradiction to the main objective, of keeping the confidential information safe, but the time, becomes unprecise.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement will terminate on the earlier of: (a) the written agreement of the parties to terminate this Agreement; (b) the consummation of the Transaction; or (c) 5 years from the date hereof.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that the confidentiality obligation lasts for '2 years from the date hereof or until the Confidential Information ceases to be a trade secret'. The changed text modifies this to 'until the termination of the Transaction'. The contradicted text states that the agreement will terminate on the earlier of (a) written agreement, (b) consummation of transaction or (c) 5 years from date hereof. Thus, changing the duration to 'termination of the transaction' creates a contradiction, because the termination could be based on consummation of the transaction, written agreement, or 5 years from the date hereof. This contradiction introduces ambiguity and is an Omission In-Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party will take reasonable steps to ensure that its Representatives adhere to the terms of this Agreement. The Receiving Party will be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by any of its Representatives.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party will encourage its Representatives to adhere to the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing the language, 'The Receiving Party will take reasonable steps to ensure that its Representatives adhere to the terms of this Agreement. The Receiving Party will be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by any of its Representatives.' with the new language 'The Receiving Party will encourage its Representatives to adhere to the terms of this Agreement.', introduces an omission that creates an internal contradiction. The first statement is strong and binding. The second one is weaker and less definitive. This contradiction becomes apparent when considering scenarios where a Representative breaches the agreement. The original clause establishes the Receiving Party's direct responsibility for such breaches. The modified clause shifts the responsibility, leaving it to the goodwill of the Receiving party.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party agrees that use or disclosure of any Confidential Information in a manner inconsistent with this Agreement will give rise to irreparable injury for which: (a) money damages may not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement by such party; (b) the other party may be entitled to specific performance and injunction and other equitable relief with respect to any such breach; (c) such remedies will not be the exclusive remedies for any such breach, but will be in addition to all other remedies available at law or in equity; and (d) in the event of litigation relating to this Agreement, if a court of competent jurisdiction determines in a final non-appealable order that one party, or any of its Representatives, has breached this Agreement, such party will be liable for reasonable legal fees and expenses incurred by the other party in connection with such litigation, including, but not limited to, any appeals.
perturbation.justification: The original text held the Receiving Party responsible for breaches by its Representatives, ensuring consistency with the 'Contradicted Text,' which outlines remedies for breaches. By changing the text to merely 'encourage' adherence, the Receiving Party's direct responsibility is removed. This creates a contradiction because the 'Contradicted Text' assumes the Receiving Party is responsible for the actions of its representatives and liable for breaches, which is no longer guaranteed under the modified clause. The omission of the Receiving Party's responsibility creates an inconsistency that undermines the enforcement mechanisms described in the Contradicted Text.

perturbation.original_text: Each party consents to the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts located in the State of Minnesota for any legal action, suit or proceeding arising out of or in connection with this Agreement. Each party further waives any objection to the laying of venue for any such suit, action or proceeding in such courts.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party agrees to mediate any disputes arising from this Agreement before pursuing other legal actions.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the clause specifying exclusive jurisdiction in Minnesota courts and replacing it with a requirement for mediation, the contract now contradicts its ability to enforce the NDA in a specific court. By removing this, there is an inconsistency, because the parties would be going to a legal action, without the jurisdiction being stated. This makes the agreement confusing.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party agrees that use or disclosure of any Confidential Information in a manner inconsistent with this Agreement will give rise to irreparable injury for which: (a) money damages may not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement by such party; (b) the other party may be entitled to specific performance and injunction and other equitable relief with respect to any such breach; (c) such remedies will not be the exclusive remedies for any such breach, but will be in addition to all other remedies available at law or in equity; and (d) in the event of litigation relating to this Agreement, if a court of competent jurisdiction determines in a final non-appealable order that one party, or any of its Representatives, has breached this Agreement, such party will be liable for reasonable legal fees and expenses incurred by the other party in connection with such litigation, including, but not limited to, any appeals.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified exclusive jurisdiction in Minnesota courts for legal actions. The changed text replaces this with a requirement for mediation. The contradicted text outlines remedies for breaches of the agreement, including litigation and legal fees. By removing the exclusive jurisdiction clause, the changed text creates a contradiction because it's unclear where litigation (as referenced in the contradicted text) would occur if mediation fails, creating uncertainty in the enforcement mechanism specified in the contradicted text. This is a strong contradiction, and adheres to Omissions - In Text Contradiction as well.
